baseline_core.title: Baseline core and already-exposed parity
baseline_core.summary: Pre-M4 baseline commands already backed by maintained library/runtime surfaces or by the internal primitives that those surfaces depend on.

dsp_levels.title: DSP and level controls
dsp_levels.summary: Missing APF/PBT/NB/monitor/VOX/mod-level style controls tracked by issue #130.

operator_toggles.title: Operator toggles and status flags
operator_toggles.summary: AGC/ANF/compressor/VOX/break-in and related toggle/status parity completed by issue #131.

vfo_dualwatch_scan.title: VFO, dual-watch, and scan control
vfo_dualwatch_scan.summary: Remaining dual-watch/scanning/VFO parity tracked by issue #132.

memory_bandstack.title: Memory and band-stacking
memory_bandstack.summary: Memory register, recall, and band-stacking work tracked by issue #133.

tone_repeater.title: Tone and repeater controls
tone_repeater.summary: Repeater tone/TSQL frequency family tracked by issue #134.

system_config.title: System and configuration
system_config.summary: Antenna, CI-V config, mod routing, and date/time/UTC work tracked by issue #135.

transceiver_status.title: Transceiver, telemetry, and RIT/TX status
transceiver_status.summary: RIT/TX status and remaining telemetry parity tracked by issue #136.

advanced_scope.title: Advanced scope controls
advanced_scope.summary: Remaining scope configuration controls tracked by issue #137.

commands.name: Freq (TRX)
commands.notes: Unsolicited transceive updates are projected into StateCache and RadioState.

commands.name: Mode (TRX)
commands.notes: Unsolicited transceive updates are projected into StateCache and RadioState.

commands.name: Band Edge Freq
commands.notes: Read-only builder; response uses standard frequency BCD parsing.

commands.name: VFO Dual Watch Off
commands.notes: Dual-watch off is only observed through CI-V receive-state projection; there is no dedicated public API yet.

commands.name: VFO Dual Watch On
commands.notes: Dual-watch on is only observed through CI-V receive-state projection; there is no dedicated public API yet.

commands.name: VFO Dual Watch
commands.notes: Dual-watch status is tracked in RadioState, but not exposed through a maintained public method/CLI/Web command family yet.

commands.name: VFO Main/Sub Band
commands.notes: Active MAIN/SUB receiver state is projected into RadioState, but there is no dedicated parity API for the 0x07/0xD2 family yet.

commands.name: Speech
commands.notes: Speech voice synthesizer (0x13). Fire-and-forget SET only (0=all, 1=freq, 2=mode).

commands.name: Mic Gain
commands.cmd29_rationale: Transceiver-global microphone gain. Per IC-7610 CI-V Reference p.3, the row carries no Command29 marker; verified against wfview IC-7610.rig (Mic Gain, Command29=false). Excluded from rigs/ic7610.toml cmd29 routes.

commands.name: Key Speed
commands.cmd29_rationale: Transceiver-global CW key speed. PDF p.3 shows no Command29 marker; wfview IC-7610.rig sets Command29=false. Excluded from rigs/ic7610.toml cmd29 routes.

commands.name: Notch Filter
commands.cmd29_rationale: IC-7610 CI-V Reference Guide Rev.4 p.3 renders the Command29 glyph on this row; the PDF is the primary IC-7610 reference and takes precedence over wfview IC-7610.rig (which records Command29=false, mirrored in `command29` above as the raw wfview snapshot). Routed in rigs/ic7610.toml cmd29 routes so each receiver keeps an independent notch position on dual-RX — otherwise MAIN and SUB would share a single notch, a real functional limitation (issue #708 review).

commands.name: Compressor Level
commands.cmd29_rationale: Transceiver-global speech-compressor gain. PDF p.3 shows no Command29 marker; wfview IC-7610.rig sets Command29=false. Excluded from rigs/ic7610.toml cmd29 routes.

commands.name: Break-In Delay
commands.cmd29_rationale: Transceiver-global CW break-in delay. PDF p.3 shows no Command29 marker; wfview IC-7610.rig sets Command29=false. Excluded from rigs/ic7610.toml cmd29 routes.

commands.name: Drive Gain
commands.cmd29_rationale: Transceiver-global DRIVE gain (TX path). PDF p.3 shows no Command29 marker; wfview IC-7610.rig sets Command29=false. Excluded from rigs/ic7610.toml cmd29 routes.

commands.name: Monitor Gain
commands.cmd29_rationale: Transceiver-global MONI audio level. PDF p.3 shows no Command29 marker; wfview IC-7610.rig sets Command29=false. Excluded from rigs/ic7610.toml cmd29 routes.

commands.name: Vox Gain
commands.cmd29_rationale: Transceiver-global VOX gain (TX path). PDF p.3 shows no Command29 marker; wfview IC-7610.rig sets Command29=false. Excluded from rigs/ic7610.toml cmd29 routes.

commands.name: Anti-Vox Gain
commands.cmd29_rationale: Transceiver-global Anti-VOX gain (TX path). PDF p.3 shows no Command29 marker; wfview IC-7610.rig sets Command29=false. Excluded from rigs/ic7610.toml cmd29 routes.

commands.name: S Meter Sql Status
commands.notes: Official IC-7610 CI-V guide documents this wire as a read-only status response despite wfview marking set=true.

commands.name: Overflow Status
commands.notes: Official IC-7610 CI-V guide documents this wire as a read-only status response despite wfview marking set=true.

commands.name: AGC Status
commands.notes: wfview labels 0x16 0x12 as AGC status; the IC-7610 CI-V guide defines the payload as AGC mode (FAST/MID/SLOW).

commands.name: Filter Shape
commands.notes: wfview advertises a broader numeric range, but the IC-7610 CI-V guide defines 0x16 0x56 as a two-state SHARP/SOFT selector.

commands.name: Transceiver ID
commands.notes: Transceiver ID (0x19 0x00). GET only, returns 1-byte model ID (IC-7610=0x98).

commands.name: RX Frequency
commands.notes: Dual-receiver RX frequency responses are consumed by RadioState/web runtime rather than a standalone public getter.

commands.name: RX Mode
commands.notes: Dual-receiver RX mode responses are consumed by RadioState/web runtime rather than a standalone public getter.

commands.name: Scope On/Off
commands.notes: High-level scope lifecycle covers this family; disable is currently implemented via data-output gating rather than a dedicated hard-off workflow.

commands.name: Main/Sub Prefix
commands.notes: Command29 prefixing is a maintained internal primitive used by exposed dual-receiver commands.

pdf_only_cmd29_rows.name: OVF Status (per-Rx)
pdf_only_cmd29_rows.cmd29_rationale: IC-7610 CI-V Reference p.8 renders the Command29 glyph on 0x1A 0x0A (per-receiver Overflow indicator), but wfview IC-7610.rig has no matching entry (only 0x1A 0x09 AF Mute carries Command29=true). Not mirrored in the commands array because the wfview-parity invariant requires 1:1 ID alignment. Library currently reads the transceiver-global OVF flag via 0x15 0x07 (command id 50); no route added to rigs/ic7610.toml pending hardware verification.
